questions.question: Aisha drinks 1/4 of a bottle of water before lunch and another 1/4 after lunch. What fraction of the bottle has she drunk altogether?
questions.explanation: Aisha drinks 1/4 + 1/4 = 2/4. Simplify: 2/4 = 1/2 of the bottle. ✓

questions.question: Which fraction is equivalent to 3/6?
questions.explanation: Simplify 3/6 by dividing both numbers by 3: 3÷3 = 1 and 6÷3 = 2, so 3/6 = 1/2. ✓

questions.question: There are 20 children in a swimming class. 3/4 of them can swim a full length. How many children can swim a full length?
Which of these fractions is the largest? 2/5, 3/10, 1/2, 3/5, 4/10
A bowl is 5/8 full of flour. Jack uses 1/8 of the bowl for a recipe. What fraction of the bowl is left?
questions.explanation: Jack has 5/8 and uses 1/8: 5/8 - 1/8 = 4/8. Simplify: 4/8 = 1/2 of the bowl left. ✓

questions.question: In a class of 16 pupils, 12 bought their lunch from the canteen. What fraction bought their lunch? Give your answer in its simplest form.
A football club has 18 members. 2/3 of them turn up for Saturday practice. How many members turn up?
Ella fills 3/5 of her water bottle at home and adds another 1/5 at school. What fraction of the bottle is full now?
questions.explanation: Add the numerators: 3/5 + 1/5 = 4/5 of the bottle is full. ✓

questions.question: A pizza is cut into 8 equal slices. Tom eats 3 slices. What fraction of the pizza is left?
Two jugs are the same size. One is 2/3 full of squash and the other is 3/4 full. Which jug has more squash?
questions.explanation: Convert to twelfths: 2/3 = 8/12 and 3/4 = 9/12. Since 9/12 > 8/12, then 3/4 is larger. ✓

questions.question: Lily eats 1/2 of a cake on Saturday and 1/4 on Sunday. What fraction of the cake has she eaten altogether?
questions.explanation: Convert to quarters: 1/2 = 2/4. Then 2/4 + 1/4 = 3/4 of the cake eaten. ✓

questions.question: In a pack of 24 coloured pencils, 18 are sharp. What fraction of the pencils are sharp? Give your answer in its simplest form.
Noah has 8 metres of ribbon. He uses 3/4 of it for a craft project. How many metres does he use?
Sarah has 35 paperback books and 20 hardback books. What fraction of her books are hardbacks?
questions.explanation: Total books = 35 + 20 = 55. Hardbacks = 20/55. Simplify: 20÷5 = 4 and 55÷5 = 11, so 4/11. ✓

questions.question: A baker makes 30 biscuits. He gives 2/5 of them to his neighbours. How many biscuits does he give away?
In a bag of 60 sweets, 15 are red. What fraction are red?
Which of these fractions is closest to 1? 7/8, 5/6, 9/10, 4/5, 11/12
questions.explanation: Distance from 1: 7/8 = 1/8 away, 5/6 = 1/6 away, 9/10 = 1/10 away, 4/5 = 1/5 away, 11/12 = 1/12 away. Smallest distance is 1/12, so 11/12 is closest. ✓

questions.question: Convert 0.6 to a fraction in its simplest form.
A chocolate bar has 24 pieces. Emily eats 1/3 of it. How many pieces does she eat?
Harry walks 2/3 of a mile to school and then 1/6 of a mile to the park. What fraction of a mile has he walked altogether?
questions.explanation: Convert to sixths: 2/3 = 4/6. Then 4/6 + 1/6 = 5/6 of a mile in total. ✓

questions.question: In a class of 30 pupils, 20 have a school dinner. What fraction have a school dinner? Give your answer in its simplest form.
A school choir has 45 members. 4/5 of them attend a concert. How many members attend?
questions.explanation: Find 1/5 of 45 first: 45 ÷ 5 = 9. Then 4/5 = 4 × 9 = 36 members attend. ✓

questions.question: A jug is 7/8 full of orange juice. Dad pours out 3/8 of the jug. What fraction of the jug still has juice?
questions.explanation: The jug had 7/8 and Dad poured out 3/8: 7/8 - 3/8 = 4/8. Simplify: 4/8 = 1/2 of the jug left. ✓

questions.question: In a test with 50 questions, Jake answered 40 correctly. What fraction did he get correct?
In a garden, 1/3 is planted with vegetables and 1/6 with herbs. What fraction of the garden is planted altogether?
questions.explanation: Convert to sixths: 1/3 = 2/6. Then 2/6 + 1/6 = 3/6. Simplify: 3/6 = 1/2 of the garden is planted. ✓

questions.question: There are 12 eggs in a box. Mum uses 5/6 of them to make a cake. How many eggs does she use?
A ribbon is 3/4 metre long. How many centimetres is this?
Ravi eats 1/3 of a pie at lunch and another 1/3 after school. What fraction of the pie has he eaten?
A quiz asks: which fraction is equivalent to 4/10?
There are 35 children in a year group. 2/5 of them sign up for the school play. How many children sign up?
In a baking contest, 15 out of 25 cakes are chocolate. What is 15/25 in its simplest form?
A craft box has 24 coloured buttons. Priya uses 3/8 of them for her project. How many buttons does she use?
On a spelling test, Amara gets 16 out of 20 correct. What is 16/20 in its simplest form?
Which is larger: 3/5 or 5/8?
questions.explanation: Convert to 40ths: 3/5 = 24/40 and 5/8 = 25/40. Since 25/40 > 24/40, then 5/8 is larger. ✓

questions.question: Maya paints 2/3 of a fence in the morning and 1/12 more after lunch. What fraction of the fence has she painted?
A cake is cut into 12 equal pieces. Lucy eats 5 pieces. What fraction is left?
A tin of paint is 5/6 full. Ruby uses 1/6 of the tin for her art project. What fraction of the tin is left?
questions.explanation: The tin had 5/6 and Ruby used 1/6: 5/6 - 1/6 = 4/6. Simplify: 4/6 = 2/3 of the tin left. ✓

questions.question: At a school sports day, 14 out of 21 events are running races. What is 14/21 in its simplest form?
Emma has 60 stickers. She gives away 3/5 of them. How many does she give away?
Jake drinks 1/6 of a carton of juice before school and 1/3 at break time. What fraction has he drunk altogether?
There are 32 children going on a school trip. 7/8 of them bring a packed lunch. How many children bring a packed lunch?
questions.explanation: 7/8 of 32 = (7 × 32) ÷ 8 = 224 ÷ 8 = 28 children bring a packed lunch. ✓

questions.question: Tom has read 5/8 of a 160-page book. How many pages has he read?
Which is larger: 2/5 or 3/7?
questions.explanation: Convert to 35ths: 2/5 = 14/35 and 3/7 = 15/35. Since 15/35 > 14/35, then 3/7 is larger. ✓

questions.question: A roll of sticky tape is 8/12 full. After wrapping a present, 2/12 has been used. What fraction is left?
There are 48 crayons in a box. Leah uses 5/12 of them for her drawing. How many crayons does she use?
A recipe needs 2/3 cup of sugar. Sophie has 1/6 cup. How much more does she need?
questions.explanation: Convert to sixths: 2/3 = 4/6. She needs 4/6 - 1/6 = 3/6. Simplify: 3/6 = 1/2 cup. ✓

questions.question: On sports day, 20 races are planned. 3/4 of them are completed before it rains. How many races are completed?
Ben travels 3/8 of a 64 km journey. How far has he travelled?
A netball club has 45 members. 2/5 of them are in Year 5. How many members are in Year 5?
A garden is 3/4 acre. What decimal is this?
Freya walks 1/5 of a mile to the shops and 3/10 of a mile back a different way. What fraction of a mile does she walk in total?
A PE teacher has 33 tennis balls. She puts 4/11 of them into a basket. How many balls are in the basket?
Mia drinks 2/7 of a 700 ml bottle. How much does she drink?
In a class of 32 students, 24 passed a test. What fraction passed?
Convert 0.4 to a fraction in its simplest form.
A jug holds 2 litres. How much is 3/4 of the jug in millilitres?
A recipe makes 50 biscuits. Oscar gives 7/10 of them to his friends. How many biscuits does he give away?
In a bag of 45 marbles, 18 are blue. What fraction are blue?
Grace eats 1/4 of a chocolate bar after school and 1/8 after dinner. What fraction of the bar has she eaten in total?
questions.explanation: Convert to eighths: 1/4 = 2/8. Then 2/8 + 1/8 = 3/8 of the bar eaten. ✓

questions.question: Oliver has read 5/6 of his book. His sister has read 1/3 of the same book. How much more of the book has Oliver read than his sister?
questions.explanation: Convert to sixths: 1/3 = 2/6. Then 5/6 - 2/6 = 3/6. Simplify: 3/6 = 1/2 more of the book. ✓

questions.question: Convert 0.8 to a fraction in its simplest form.
A box of 56 chocolates has 21 dark chocolates. What fraction are dark?
A rope is 5/8 metre long. How many centimetres is this?
Zara fills 3/4 of her water bottle in the morning and adds another 1/8 at lunchtime. What fraction of the bottle is now full?
questions.explanation: Convert to eighths: 3/4 = 6/8. Then 6/8 + 1/8 = 7/8 of the bottle is full. ✓

questions.question: In a test with 80 questions, Emma answered 64 correctly. What fraction did she get correct?
Which is smaller: 4/7 or 5/9?
questions.explanation: Convert to 63rds: 4/7 = 36/63 and 5/9 = 35/63. Since 35/63 < 36/63, then 5/9 is smaller. ✓

questions.question: A bag contains 72 sweets. If 2/9 are strawberry flavour, how many are strawberry?
Convert 0.35 to a fraction in its simplest form.
Which fraction is closest to 1? 8/9, 6/7, 10/11, 5/6, 12/13
questions.explanation: Distance from 1: 8/9 = 1/9 away, 6/7 = 1/7 away, 10/11 = 1/11 away, 5/6 = 1/6 away, 12/13 = 1/13 away. Smallest distance is 1/13, so 12/13 is closest. ✓

questions.question: After eating 3/5 of his sweets, Tom has 24 left. How many sweets did he start with?
questions.explanation: If Tom ate 3/5, he has 2/5 left. 2/5 of the total = 24 sweets. So 1/5 = 24 ÷ 2 = 12 sweets. Total = 5 × 12 = 60 sweets. ✓

questions.question: A recipe uses 2/3 of a bag of flour. Each bag weighs 1.5 kg. How many grams of flour are used?
Lily has 3/4 of a pizza. She gives 2/3 of what she has to her brother. What fraction of the whole pizza does her brother get?
questions.explanation: Brother gets 2/3 of 3/4 = 2/3 × 3/4 = 6/12 = 1/2 of the whole pizza. ✓

questions.question: Sam walks 1/4 of a mile to school, 1/3 of a mile to the shops, and 5/12 of a mile home. How far does he walk in total?
In a class, 1/4 of the pupils walk to school and 30% cycle. What fraction of the class either walk or cycle?
A builder has 3 1/2 metres of wood. He uses 1 3/4 metres for a shelf. How much wood is left?
Oliver has £48. He spends 3/8 on books and 1/6 on a gift. How much does he have left?
A bag has 15 red, 10 blue, and 5 green marbles. What fraction of the marbles are NOT blue?
questions.explanation: Total marbles = 15 + 10 + 5 = 30. Not blue = 15 + 5 = 20. Fraction = 20/30. Simplify: 20 ÷ 10 = 2 and 30 ÷ 10 = 3, so 2/3. ✓

questions.question: Which of these has the greatest value? 3/8, 0.45, 37%, 2/5, 7/16
questions.explanation: Convert all to decimals: 3/8 = 0.375, 0.45 = 0.45, 37% = 0.37, 2/5 = 0.4, 7/16 = 0.4375. The greatest value is 0.45. ✓

questions.question: Priya spends 1/4 of her money on a book and 1/3 on a game. She has £25 left. How much money did she start with?
Three children share a 2.4 kg bag of oranges. Ava takes 1/3, Ben takes 3/8 of what is left, and Charlie gets the rest. How many grams does Charlie get?
questions.explanation: 2.4 kg = 2400 g. Ava takes 1/3 of 2400 = 800 g. Remaining = 1600 g. Ben takes 3/8 of 1600 = 600 g. Charlie gets 1600 - 600 = 1000 g. ✓

questions.question: A water tank is 5/6 full. Then 3/8 of the tank is drained. What fraction of the tank is still full?
Hannah runs 2 1/3 km on Monday and 1 5/6 km on Tuesday. How far does she run in total?
A school has 240 pupils. 3/8 are in Key Stage 1 and 5/12 are in lower Key Stage 2. The rest are in upper Key Stage 2. How many pupils are in upper Key Stage 2?
Tom has 24 sweets. He gives 1/4 of them to his sister. How many sweets does he give away?
A pizza has 8 slices. Ava eats 3/8 of the pizza. How many slices does she eat?
There are 30 children on a school trip. 2/5 of them bring a packed lunch. How many children bring a packed lunch?
Lily bakes 20 cupcakes. She gives 1/2 to her neighbours. How many cupcakes does she give away?
A box contains 36 coloured pencils. 1/6 of them are red. How many red pencils are there?
Sophie has £60. She spends 2/5 on a book and 1/6 on a toy. How much has she spent in total?
A jar holds 48 marbles. 3/8 are blue and 1/4 are green. How many marbles are neither blue nor green?
questions.explanation: Blue: 3/8 of 48 = 18. Green: 1/4 of 48 = 12. Neither: 48 − 18 − 12 = 18 marbles. ✓

questions.question: Jack has a 3/4 full bottle of squash. He pours out 1/3 of what is in the bottle. What fraction of the full bottle is left?
A sports club has 40 members. 3/5 are boys. Of the boys, 1/4 play football. How many boys play football?
questions.explanation: Boys: 3/5 of 40 = 24. Boys who play football: 1/4 of 24 = 6. ✓

questions.question: Oliver walks 3/4 km to school and 3/4 km home again. He does this 5 days a week. How far does he walk in a week?
Mia has 50 stickers. She gives 2/5 to her brother and 1/10 to her friend. How many stickers does she have left?
questions.explanation: Brother gets 2/5 of 50 = 20. Friend gets 1/10 of 50 = 5. Mia has 50 − 20 − 5 = 25 stickers left. ✓

questions.question: A recipe needs 3/4 kg of flour. Emily only has 1/3 kg. How much more flour does she need?
A class of 32 children vote for their favourite sport. 1/4 choose football, 3/8 choose netball, and the rest choose swimming. How many choose swimming?
questions.explanation: Football: 1/4 of 32 = 8. Netball: 3/8 of 32 = 12. Swimming: 32 − 8 − 12 = 12 children. ✓

questions.question: After eating 3/5 of the cakes, there are 12 left. How many cakes were there to start with?
questions.explanation: If 3/5 were eaten, 2/5 remain. 2/5 of the total = 12, so 1/5 = 6 and the total = 5 × 6 = 30 cakes. ✓

questions.question: Harry spends 2/5 of his pocket money on a comic and 1/4 on sweets. He has £1.75 left. How much pocket money did he start with?
A school fête raises £360. The PTA keeps 1/3 for next year. Of the rest, 3/4 goes to charity. How much goes to charity?
questions.explanation: PTA keeps 1/3 of £360 = £120. Remaining: £360 − £120 = £240. Charity gets 3/4 of £240 = £180. ✓

questions.question: A tank is 5/6 full of water. After using 2/3 of the water, what fraction of the tank still has water?
Grace reads 2/7 of her book on Monday and 1/3 on Tuesday. What fraction does she still have left to read?
Ben and Chloe share a prize. Ben gets 3/8 and Chloe gets 5/12. The remaining £45 goes back to the school. What was the total prize?
A farmer plants carrots on 2/5 of his field and potatoes on 1/4. The rest of the field is 140 square metres. What is the total area of the field?
questions.explanation: Planted: 2/5 + 1/4. Common denominator 20: 8/20 + 5/20 = 13/20. Remaining: 7/20 = 140 m². So 1/20 = 20 m² and 20/20 = 400 m². ✓

questions.question: What is 1 2/5 + 2 1/5?
questions.explanation: Add the whole numbers: 1 + 2 = 3. Add the fractions: 2/5 + 1/5 = 3/5. Total: 3 3/5. ✓

questions.question: What is 2 3/8 + 1 5/8?
questions.explanation: Add whole numbers: 2 + 1 = 3. Add fractions: 3/8 + 5/8 = 8/8 = 1. Total: 3 + 1 = 4. ✓

questions.question: What is 3/4 − 1/2?
questions.explanation: Convert to the same denominator. 1/2 = 2/4. Then 3/4 − 2/4 = 1/4. ✓

questions.question: What is 3/5 − 1/4?
questions.explanation: Find a common denominator (20). 3/5 = 12/20 and 1/4 = 5/20. Then 12/20 − 5/20 = 7/20. ✓

questions.question: What is 7/8 − 2/3?
questions.explanation: Find a common denominator (24). 7/8 = 21/24 and 2/3 = 16/24. Then 21/24 − 16/24 = 5/24. ✓

questions.question: What is 4/5 − 1/3?
questions.explanation: Find a common denominator (15). 4/5 = 12/15 and 1/3 = 5/15. Then 12/15 − 5/15 = 7/15. ✓

questions.question: What is 5/6 − 3/8?
questions.explanation: Find a common denominator (24). 5/6 = 20/24 and 3/8 = 9/24. Then 20/24 − 9/24 = 11/24. ✓

questions.question: Emily has 7/8 of a metre of ribbon. She uses 2/5 of a metre for a card. How much ribbon does she have left?
questions.explanation: Find a common denominator (40). 7/8 = 35/40 and 2/5 = 16/40. Then 35/40 − 16/40 = 19/40 m. ✓

questions.question: A bottle is 5/6 full of juice. Jack pours out 3/10 of the bottle. What fraction of the bottle still has juice?
questions.explanation: Find a common denominator (30). 5/6 = 25/30 and 3/10 = 9/30. Then 25/30 − 9/30 = 16/30. Simplify: 16/30 = 8/15. ✓

questions.question: What is 2/5 + 1/3?
questions.explanation: Find a common denominator (15). 2/5 = 6/15 and 1/3 = 5/15. Then 6/15 + 5/15 = 11/15. ✓

questions.question: What is 3/8 + 1/6?
questions.explanation: Find a common denominator (24). 3/8 = 9/24 and 1/6 = 4/24. Then 9/24 + 4/24 = 13/24. ✓

questions.question: What is 2/3 + 1/5?
questions.explanation: Find a common denominator (15). 2/3 = 10/15 and 1/5 = 3/15. Then 10/15 + 3/15 = 13/15. ✓

questions.question: Rosie walks 2/5 of a mile to school, then 1/4 of a mile to the library. How far does she walk altogether?
questions.explanation: Find a common denominator (20). 2/5 = 8/20 and 1/4 = 5/20. Then 8/20 + 5/20 = 13/20 mile. ✓

questions.question: What is 2/3 × 3/5?
questions.explanation: Multiply numerators: 2 × 3 = 6. Multiply denominators: 3 × 5 = 15. So 6/15. Simplify: 6/15 = 2/5. ✓

questions.question: What is 3/4 × 2/7?
questions.explanation: Multiply numerators: 3 × 2 = 6. Multiply denominators: 4 × 7 = 28. So 6/28. Simplify: 6/28 = 3/14. ✓

questions.question: A recipe uses 3/4 of a cup of sugar. Sophie makes 2/3 of the recipe. How much sugar does she use?
Which is greater: 5/8 or 0.6?
questions.explanation: Convert 5/8 to a decimal: 5 ÷ 8 = 0.625. Since 0.625 > 0.6, then 5/8 is greater. ✓

questions.question: Put these in order from smallest to largest: 0.3, 1/4, 2/7
Which is larger: 1/3 or 1/4?
questions.explanation: With unit fractions, the smaller the denominator, the larger the fraction. 3 < 4, so 1/3 > 1/4. ✓

questions.question: Which is smaller: 2/5 or 3/5?
questions.explanation: Both fractions have the same denominator (5). The one with the smaller numerator is smaller: 2 < 3, so 2/5 < 3/5. ✓

questions.question: Put these fractions in order from smallest to largest: 1/2, 1/4, 1/3
questions.explanation: With unit fractions, the larger the denominator, the smaller the fraction. 1/4 is the smallest, then 1/3, then 1/2 is the largest. ✓

questions.question: What is 7/20 as a percentage?
questions.explanation: To convert to a percentage, multiply by 100. 7/20 × 100 = 700 ÷ 20 = 35%. ✓
